items.title: HSAJB’s Cardiology Department Understaffed, Overworked, Lacking Equipment — Specialist
items.summary: HSAJB's cardiology department is severely understaffed and overworked, besides not having sufficient beds or angiography machines. Staff at every level are on excessive on-calls or double shifts. Johor has the lowest staffing occupancy rate in MOH. The post HSAJB’s Cardiology Department Understaffed, Overworked, Lacking Equipment — Specialist appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: HTAR Klang’s Surgery Department On Verge Of Collapse — Concerned Surgical Medical Officers
items.summary: HTAR Klang's general surgery department, with just 20 MOs serving 300-400 patients daily (inpatients/outpatients/ED), is on the verge of collapse, say surgical MOs. "Surgical services in HTAR are now operating beyond safe human and operational limits." The post HTAR Klang’s Surgery Department On Verge Of Collapse — Concerned Surgical Medical Officers appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Chaining Is Not Care: Malaysia Must Move Past Restraint And Rethink How We See Autism — Dr Amar-Singh HSS, Ng Lai-Thin & Wong Hui-Min
items.summary: Chaining a child, whatever her diagnosis, is not a clinical intervention. It is restraint, and restraint of this kind has no place in the care of a person with a disability. The post Chaining Is Not Care: Malaysia Must Move Past Restraint And Rethink How We See Autism — Dr Amar-Singh HSS, Ng Lai-Thin & Wong Hui-Min appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Harm Reduction Saves Lives, Protects Communities And Strengthens The Economy
items.summary: As Malaysia faces the rising burden of NCDs, an ageing population, and increasing health care costs, the lesson from HIV remains clear: preventing harm early is one of the smartest investments a nation can make. The post Harm Reduction Saves Lives, Protects Communities And Strengthens The Economy appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Balancing Financing And Patient Care: Accelerate Regulating TPAs And MCOs — Dr James Jeremiah
items.summary: As Malaysia aims to cement its status as a regional health care hub, robust governance is vital to offering regulatory certainty to investors, transparency to patients, and fairness to health care providers. The post Balancing Financing And Patient Care: Accelerate Regulating TPAs And MCOs — Dr James Jeremiah appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: First, Do No Harm: Why Doctors’ Professional Fees Must Be Excluded From Malaysia’s DRG Implementation — Dr Rajeentheran Suntheralingam
items.summary: Designing a system that respects the distinct roles of institutional facilities and clinical experts will allow the country to protect public affordability without compromising patient safety or clinical excellence. The post First, Do No Harm: Why Doctors’ Professional Fees Must Be Excluded From Malaysia’s DRG Implementation — Dr Rajeentheran Suntheralingam appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Government Names Base MHIT ‘MediAsas’, Waiting Periods Uncertain
items.summary: The government-designed Base MHIT, named MediAsas, will be offered by six ITOs during its pilot in the Klang Valley from end-July to October. MOH didn't specify waiting periods; conventional medical plans have 120-day waiting periods for certain illnesses. The post Government Names Base MHIT ‘MediAsas’, Waiting Periods Uncertain appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Government Spends More On Innovator Medicines Than Generics, Says Pharmaniaga
items.summary: Pharmaniaga says 59% of the government's pharmaceutical spending in 2025 went to innovators (RM2.1b), compared to 41% on generics (RM1.4b). The government also allocated two-thirds of its budget on imported drugs, be it originators or imported generics. The post Government Spends More On Innovator Medicines Than Generics, Says Pharmaniaga appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Community Pharmacies Want Integration Into MOH’s New Emergency Triage System
items.summary: MPS suggests integrating community pharmacies into MOH's new emergency triage system for government hospitals, in which less urgent or routine cases with minor ailments can be referred to participating community pharmacists for assessment and management. The post Community Pharmacies Want Integration Into MOH’s New Emergency Triage System appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Coping Without A Limb: Realities Facing Amputees Today — Dr Noor Hasliza Che Seman, Dr Che Chong Chin & Nithya Roobini Govindarajoo
items.summary: People living with limb loss should not be defined by what they have lost, but recognised for their abilities, resilience, and potential to contribute meaningfully to society. The post Coping Without A Limb: Realities Facing Amputees Today — Dr Noor Hasliza Che Seman, Dr Che Chong Chin & Nithya Roobini Govindarajoo appeared first on CodeBlue .
